chunk_summary: This section provides concise 30-second caregiver counseling scripts for common questions about fortified rice, iodized salt, weekly IFA, therapeutic iron, and zinc adherence in India's national nutrition programs.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of fortified rice, iodized salt, and zinc supplementation in preventing nutritional deficiencies.
Learn the distinction between weekly IFA tablets for prevention and therapeutic iron for anemia treatment.
Recognize proper adherence guidelines for zinc supplementation during diarrhea management.
key_facts: Fortified rice contains fortified kernels blended at a ratio of 1:100 and should be cooked normally without discarding water.
Iodized salt prevents thyroid and brain-development issues; it must be kept dry and away from heat and moisture.
Weekly IFA tablets are for prevention, while therapeutic iron doses are weight-based and prescribed for anemia treatment.
Zinc supplementation requires 14 consecutive days of adherence, even if diarrhea resolves earlier.
ORS prevents dehydration, while zinc reduces diarrhea recurrence and shortens illness duration.
reference_quote: Fortified rice is regular rice with a tiny share of rice-shaped fortified kernels blended in (about 1:100). It adds small nutrients to help prevent deficiencies across the community. (Source: Compendium of Food Fortification Regulations, FSSAI)
document_key_topics: Fortified rice and iodized salt usage guidelines
Weekly IFA versus therapeutic iron for anemia prevention and treatment
Zinc supplementation adherence during diarrhea management
